text:
HEMATOPOIETIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANT INPATIENT PROGRESS NOTE
Day +18 Post-Allogeneic Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplant
Attending: BMT Attending, Hematology/Oncology Division

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR CONTINUED HOSPITALIZATION:
Ms. Margaret Okonkwo is a 48-year-old woman with AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN 2022 adverse risk, who is now day +18 following a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor (MUD) allogeneic peripheral blood stem cell transplant. She is admitted for ongoing post-transplant monitoring. This note documents her day +18 inpatient assessment and engraftment confirmation.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Okonkwo is a 48-year-old Nigerian-American woman and licensed practical nurse with a history of hypertension, type 2 diabetes mellitus, and antecedent myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS, RCMD subtype, diagnosed approximately 18 months prior to AML transformation) who presented approximately 4 months ago with a new diagnosis of AML-MRC. At presentation, she had leukocytosis to 84,000/uL, severe anemia (Hgb 7.1), severe thrombocytopenia (platelets 28,000), and marked symptomatic disease including fatigue, dyspnea on exertion, gingival bleeding, and low-grade fevers. Bone marrow biopsy confirmed 62% myeloblasts with dysplastic features, and NGS demonstrated a high-allelic-ratio FLT3-ITD (VAF 82%) alongside co-mutations in DNMT3A (R882H), ASXL1, SRSF2, and RUNX1 — an adverse-risk genomic landscape per ELN 2022 criteria.

She received standard 7+3 induction chemotherapy (cytarabine 200 mg/m2/day days 1-7 continuous infusion plus daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 IV days 1-3) with midostaurin 50 mg BID added on days 8-21 per the RATIFY protocol given FLT3-ITD positivity. Induction was complicated by E. coli bacteremia and febrile neutropenia managed successfully with cefepime, as well as grade 3 mucositis. Day +30 bone marrow biopsy confirmed morphologic complete remission with MRD negativity by 8-color flow cytometry (sensitivity 0.01%), and FLT3-ITD was undetectable by PCR. She was discharged and continued midostaurin during the outpatient consolidation planning phase.

Given adverse-risk disease with multiple co-mutations and AML-MRC features, alloHSCT in CR1 was strongly recommended. HLA typing was initiated immediately; no matched sibling donor was available, and an unrelated donor search via the NMDP/Be The Match registry identified a 10/10 HLA allele-level matched unrelated male donor (CMV seronegative, D-/R+ mismatch) within approximately 8 weeks. Prior to transplant, she received one cycle of consolidation with high-dose cytarabine (HiDAC 3 g/m2 q12h days 1, 3, 5) plus midostaurin days 8-21. HiDAC was complicated by grade 2 cerebellar neurotoxicity (ataxia, dysmetria) on day 6, requiring early discontinuation after 5 of 6 planned doses, with full recovery without lasting neurologic deficit. Pre-transplant restaging PET-CT and repeat bone marrow biopsy at approximately 3.5 months from diagnosis confirmed sustained MRD-negative complete remission and no extramedullary disease; she was cleared for transplant.

Approximately 4 months after initial AML diagnosis, she underwent MUD alloHSCT with reduced-intensity conditioning (RIC): fludarabine 30 mg/m2/day IV on days -6 through -2 plus busulfan 3.2 mg/kg/day IV on days -3 and -2 (IV busulfan with pharmacokinetic targeting, AUC ~5,000 uM*min/day). GVHD prophylaxis consisted of tacrolimus (target trough 8-12 ng/mL), methotrexate (15 mg/m2 day +1, then 10 mg/m2 on days +3, +6, +11), and rabbit anti-thymocyte globulin (Thymoglobulin 2.5 mg/kg/day on days -3, -2, -1). Stem cells were peripheral blood stem cells with a CD34+ cell dose of 5.8 x 10^6/kg; infusion on day 0 proceeded without acute infusion reaction. Midostaurin was discontinued prior to conditioning.

She has been monitored daily since transplant. Today is day +18, and we are confirming engraftment. The past several days have been notable for gradual neutrophil recovery — ANC first exceeded 500/uL on day +16 and has remained above 500/uL for now 3 consecutive days, meeting the institutional definition of neutrophil engraftment. Platelet counts are trending upward, with the most recent unsupported platelet count approaching 20,000/uL (platelet engraftment criterion not yet fully met, closely anticipated over the next 1-2 days). She has been afebrile for 5 days. She tolerated tacrolimus and prophylactic medications without significant adverse effects. She has no oral mucosal lesions, no skin rash, and no GI complaints. She denies nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or abdominal cramping. No right upper quadrant pain, no lower extremity edema, no significant weight change compared to baseline. She is eating well orally. She reports feeling markedly better than at any point during the prior hospitalizations.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
1. AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN adverse risk — as above; currently post-MUD alloHSCT day +18, in morphologic and MRD-negative CR1 at last assessment
2. Myelodysplastic syndrome (RCMD) — antecedent, identified 18 months prior to AML transformation; managed with observation, now considered part of the AML-MRC disease history
3. Hypertension — currently managed with lisinopril 10 mg daily (continued through transplant admission, held on days with hemodynamic variability as needed)
4. Type 2 diabetes mellitus — managed with metformin 1000 mg BID prior to admission; metformin held during inpatient stay secondary to contrast exposure risk and renal monitoring requirements; glucose management with sliding scale insulin during transplant hospitalization; HbA1c 7.1% at last check prior to admission
5. E. coli bacteremia — complication of induction febrile neutropenia; treated with cefepime 14-day course, no residual sequelae
6. Cytarabine-related cerebellar neurotoxicity (grade 2) — complication of HiDAC consolidation, fully resolved; neurology follow-up completed, no lasting neurologic deficit

---

PAST SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Right arm PICC line placed for induction chemotherapy; removed prior to consolidation
- Central venous catheter (tunneled Hickman, double-lumen) placed for transplant conditioning and infusion; currently in situ, functioning well
- Bone marrow biopsies x 4 (diagnosis, day +30 post-induction, pre-transplant, and pending day +30 post-transplant)
- No prior major surgeries

---

SOCIAL HISTORY:
Ms. Okonkwo is originally from Lagos, Nigeria, and has lived in the United States for 20 years. She is married with two adult children. She works as a licensed practical nurse at a community hospital but has been on medical leave since her AML diagnosis. She is a lifelong never-smoker. She consumes alcohol very rarely (occasional social use, less than 1 drink per month). She denies recreational drug use. She lives in a single-family home with her husband, who has been highly engaged as her primary caregiver. Her home environment is being assessed for appropriateness for post-discharge care. She has strong family and community support.

---

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No known family history of hematologic malignancy, AML, MDS, or lymphoma
- Mother: hypertension, type 2 diabetes mellitus (died of cardiovascular disease in her 70s)
- Father: alive, age 76, with hypertension and benign prostatic hyperplasia
- Siblings: two sisters and one brother; none with known malignancy; HLA typing performed — no matched sibling identified as potential donor
- No known hereditary cancer syndromes (no Li-Fraumeni, no Fanconi anemia history by family report)

---

ALLERGIES:
- No known drug allergies (NKDA)
- No known food or latex allergies

---

MEDICATIONS (Current Inpatient):
1. Tacrolimus — oral, titrated to trough 8-12 ng/mL; current dose 2 mg BID (most recent trough 9.4 ng/mL, therapeutic)
2. Methotrexate — IV GVHD prophylaxis doses completed (day +1, +3, +6, +11); no further doses scheduled
3. Voriconazole — antifungal prophylaxis, 200 mg PO BID; monitoring voriconazole levels given tacrolimus co-administration (dose-adjusted to minimize calcineurin inhibitor interaction)
4. Acyclovir — HSV/VZV prophylaxis, 400 mg PO TID
5. Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (TMP-SMX) — PCP prophylaxis, single-strength tablet PO three times weekly; held during pre-engraftment period, being reinitiated now that ANC recovering (per protocol, restart at ANC >1000/uL)
6. Ursodiol — hepatoprotective, VOD/SOS prophylaxis, 300 mg PO BID; to be continued through day +100 per institutional protocol
7. Lisinopril — 10 mg PO daily (hypertension)
8. Insulin glargine — 8 units SQ at bedtime (steroid-related and illness-related glucose management); regular insulin sliding scale per BMT nursing protocol
9. Ondansetron — 4 mg IV/PO q8h PRN nausea
10. Lorazepam — 0.5 mg IV/PO PRN for refractory nausea or anxiety
11. Magnesium oxide — 400 mg PO daily (tacrolimus-associated hypomagnesemia prophylaxis; repletion as needed)
12. Multivitamin (no iron) — daily
13. Stool softener (docusate sodium) — 100 mg PO BID

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Constitutional: Fatigued but markedly improved from nadir; no fevers for 5 days; no chills; no drenching night sweats; appetite returning, eating approximately 80% of meals orally
Head/Eyes: No blurry vision, no eye pain or discharge; no icterus
ENT: Mild residual oral dryness (likely methotrexate and ATG effect); no oropharyngeal ulcers; no sore throat
Respiratory: Mild exertional dyspnea with ambulation (expected in peri-engraftment period); no cough; no hemoptysis; no chest pain with breathing; resting oxygen saturation adequate on room air
Cardiovascular: No chest pain; no palpitations; mild lower extremity edema (bilateral, 1+, likely related to IV fluid administration during conditioning/early post-transplant period)
Gastrointestinal: Soft formed bowel movements daily; no diarrhea, no blood in stool, no abdominal cramping or pain; no nausea at this time; no early satiety; no dysphagia
Genitourinary: Urinating without difficulty; no hematuria; no dysuria; adequate urine output
Musculoskeletal: Generalized deconditioning and weakness, expected; no joint pain, no swelling
Skin: No rashes, no new skin lesions, no pruritis; no jaundice; no palmar erythema
Neurologic: No new headache; no focal deficits; no ataxia (prior cytarabine-related ataxia fully resolved months ago)
Psychiatric: Anxious about recovery trajectory but overall coping well; supported by husband and family; social work consulted
Endocrine: Blood glucose somewhat elevated at times, managed with insulin; no polyuria beyond expected

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vital Signs: Temperature 37.0 degrees C (afebrile x 5 days); Blood pressure 132/84 mmHg; Heart rate 88 beats/min and regular; Respiratory rate 16/min; Oxygen saturation 97% on room air; Weight 74.2 kg (baseline pre-transplant 73.8 kg; weight gain < 1%)
General: Alert, awake, and oriented x 3. In no acute distress. Appears fatigued but interactive and conversational. Mood improved compared to earlier in hospitalization.
Head: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Mild alopecia (expected from conditioning chemotherapy).
Eyes: Pupils equal, round, reactive to light. Sclerae anicteric. No conjunctival pallor. No chemosis.
ENT: Oropharynx moist. No oral mucosal ulcers, erythema, or lichenoid changes. Mild coating of tongue (likely thrush-free given voriconazole prophylaxis; no clinical thrush identified). Mild xerostomia noted by patient. Tunneled central venous catheter (Hickman) right chest — exit site clean and dry, no erythema, no discharge, dressing intact.
Neck: Supple. No lymphadenopathy. No JVD.
Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No crackles, no wheezes, no rhonchi. No dullness to percussion.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. S1 and S2 normal. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. Mild bilateral lower extremity pitting edema 1+ to the mid-shin bilaterally.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Normal active bowel sounds in all quadrants. No hepatomegaly (liver span 10 cm by percussion). No splenomegaly palpable (prior splenomegaly has resolved on imaging). No ascites. No right upper quadrant tenderness (no concern for VOD/SOS).
Extremities: Mild bilateral lower extremity edema as noted. No erythema, warmth, or cord suggesting DVT. No clubbing or cyanosis.
Skin: No rashes. No maculopapular eruption. No palmar or plantar erythema. No jaundice. No petechiae or ecchymoses (platelets recovering). Mild residual alopecia.
Neurologic: Alert and oriented. Cranial nerves II-XII intact. No focal motor deficits. Normal finger-nose-finger testing (no dysmetria; prior cerebellar toxicity fully resolved). Gait mildly unsteady secondary to deconditioning but not ataxic; requires standby assist.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (Most Recent, Day +18):
Complete Blood Count:
- WBC: 1,240/uL (trending upward from nadir)
- ANC: 680/uL (day +18; first ANC >500/uL was day +16 — engraftment criterion met)
- Hemoglobin: 9.2 g/dL (transfusion threshold Hgb 7.5-8 g/dL; no pRBC needed today)
- MCV: 102 fL (macrocytic pattern, expected post-conditioning)
- Platelets: 18,000/uL (unsupported; trending up from nadir of ~5,000; prophylactic platelet transfusion threshold at 10,000, supportive transfusions administered as needed)
- Absolute lymphocyte count: 240/uL (recovering)
- Absolute monocyte count: 180/uL (recovering)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium 138 mEq/L, Potassium 3.8 mEq/L (repleted as needed), Chloride 102 mEq/L, Bicarbonate 24 mEq/L
- BUN 18 mg/dL, Creatinine 0.91 mg/dL (baseline ~0.85; renal function well-preserved)
- Glucose 148 mg/dL (mildly elevated; insulin sliding scale in use)
- Total bilirubin 0.9 mg/dL (NORMAL — no hyperbilirubinemia; important in context of VOD/SOS monitoring)
- Direct bilirubin 0.3 mg/dL
- ALT 28 U/L, AST 32 U/L (both normal; no transaminase elevation)
- Alkaline phosphatase 72 U/L (normal)
- Albumin 3.2 g/dL (mild hypoalbuminemia, expected in post-transplant inflammatory state)

Other:
- Tacrolimus trough: 9.4 ng/mL (therapeutic range 8-12 ng/mL — within goal)
- Magnesium: 1.6 mEq/L (low-normal; oral magnesium supplementation ongoing)
- Phosphorus: 2.8 mg/dL (low-normal; repleting)
- CMV PCR (peripheral blood): NEGATIVE — no CMV DNAemia detected at this time (D-/R+ mismatch; weekly surveillance ongoing)
- EBV PCR: not sent (low risk at this stage; will monitor per protocol)
- Uric acid: within normal limits
- LDH: 218 U/L (mildly elevated, consistent with ongoing marrow regeneration and engraftment activity; down from peak of 890 U/L at diagnosis)

Blood cultures (peripheral and central): No growth to date (3 days since last set drawn on day +15 in setting of low-grade fever that resolved; no current fever)

Voriconazole level: 2.1 mg/L (within therapeutic range 1-5.5 mg/L; tacrolimus dose adjusted accordingly)

---

IMAGING RESULTS:
No new imaging obtained today. Chest radiograph performed on day +14 (not repeated today given no respiratory symptoms or new fevers): Clear lung fields, no new infiltrates, no pleural effusions, no vascular congestion. Hickman line tip in appropriate position. Pre-transplant CT C/A/P (approximately day -10 prior to conditioning) demonstrated no extramedullary AML and resolved splenomegaly compared to diagnosis. Pre-transplant PET-CT confirmed no metabolically active disease. No new imaging indicated at this time.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

Ms. Margaret Okonkwo is a 48-year-old woman with AML-MRC, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN 2022 adverse risk, who achieved MRD-negative CR1 after 7+3 plus midostaurin, and who is now on day +18 following 10/10 MUD RIC-alloHSCT (flu/bu + ATG). Today's assessment is overall very encouraging.

1. NEUTROPHIL ENGRAFTMENT — CONFIRMED
ANC first exceeded 500/uL on day +16 and has remained above this threshold for 3 consecutive days, meeting institutional and standard transplant criteria for neutrophil engraftment. Engraftment day is recorded as day +16. This is a reassuring timeline for a RIC conditioning regimen with PBSC graft and ATG. Platelet engraftment anticipated within the next several days — current unsupported platelet count approaching 20,000/uL (platelet engraftment criterion: >20,000/uL unsupported for 3 consecutive days). Will continue to follow daily CBC. No interventions planned specifically for platelet engraftment other than continued supportive transfusion as needed (threshold 10,000/uL or <20,000/uL with active bleeding).

2. VENO-OCCLUSIVE DISEASE (VOD/SOS) — NO EVIDENCE
Liver function tests are entirely normal today (total bilirubin 0.9, ALT 28, AST 32, ALP 72). No hepatomegaly on exam. No right upper quadrant tenderness. No significant weight gain (< 0.5% above baseline). No ascites. Patient is not meeting any modified Seattle or EBMT criteria for VOD/SOS. Ursodiol 300 mg BID for prophylaxis is being continued through day +100. No defibrotide indicated at this time.

3. ACUTE GRAFT-VERSUS-HOST DISEASE (aGVHD) — GRADE 0 AT PRESENT
Careful daily assessment ongoing. Skin: no maculopapular rash, no erythema consistent with aGVHD. GI: no diarrhea, no nausea/vomiting of GVHD pattern, normal bowel pattern. Liver: LFTs normal, bilirubin normal. At this time there is no clinical evidence of acute GVHD in any organ system (grade 0 overall). It is important to note that with ATG-based GVHD prophylaxis and RIC conditioning, the time course of aGVHD may be somewhat delayed. We will remain vigilant. Tacrolimus trough is currently 9.4 ng/mL — within the therapeutic target range of 8-12 ng/mL. No dose adjustments needed today. Methotrexate GVHD prophylaxis doses are complete (last dose given on day +11).

4. INFECTIOUS DISEASE PROPHYLAXIS AND SURVEILLANCE
- CMV: Weekly CMV PCR surveillance initiated. Today's result is negative. D-/R+ CMV serostatus mismatch places this patient at moderate-to-high risk for CMV reactivation post-engraftment, particularly as immunosuppression continues. Will maintain weekly surveillance through at minimum day +100, per protocol. No pre-emptive therapy indicated at this time.
- Fungal prophylaxis: Voriconazole 200 mg PO BID ongoing; level therapeutic at 2.1 mg/L. Tacrolimus dose adjusted given CYP3A4 inhibition by voriconazole.
- Bacterial: Patient afebrile x 5 days. No active antibiotic therapy at present. Blood cultures from day +15 (sent in setting of low-grade fever to 38.1 degrees) with no growth; fever has resolved. No new infectious concerns today. Central line exit site clean. Will restart TMP-SMX (PCP prophylaxis) today given ANC now >1000/uL; single-strength three times weekly per protocol.
- HSV/VZV: Acyclovir 400 mg PO TID continued.
- No clinical or laboratory evidence of EBV-related lymphoproliferative process at this time; EBV PCR to be checked per protocol at day +30.

5. CHIMERISM TESTING
Whole-blood and lineage-specific chimerism (STR analysis) was sent on day +14 from peripheral blood; results pending. Bone marrow chimerism will be performed at the day +30 protocol biopsy. We anticipate high-level donor engraftment given robust and early recovery, PBSC graft source, and RIC conditioning. Results will be reviewed and discussed at day +30 assessment.

6. DISEASE STATUS / MRD MONITORING
Patient was in MRD-negative CR1 (by 8-color flow cytometry and FLT3-ITD digital PCR) at both the day +30 post-induction and pre-transplant bone marrow biopsies. No circulating blasts on current peripheral smear. Formal MRD assessment will be performed at the day +30 post-transplant bone marrow biopsy per protocol. Given the adverse-risk mutational profile (FLT3-ITD, DNMT3A, ASXL1, SRSF2, RUNX1), molecular MRD tracking including FLT3-ITD digital PCR and DNMT3A R882H ddPCR will be included at the day +30 biopsy.

7. FLUID STATUS / ELECTROLYTES
Mild bilateral lower extremity edema (1+) present, attributable to IV fluid administration during conditioning and early post-transplant period. Weight stable. No clinical or radiographic evidence of volume overload. Will reduce maintenance IV fluids and encourage oral hydration now that patient tolerating PO well. Magnesium mildly low (1.6 mEq/L) — continue oral magnesium supplementation; IV repletion if drops below 1.4 mEq/L. Phosphorus mildly low — oral replete as needed. Continue monitoring BMP daily.

8. GLYCEMIC MANAGEMENT
Glucose mildly elevated today (148 mg/dL) but acceptable. Insulin glargine 8 units at bedtime with sliding scale regular insulin. Metformin held during inpatient stay per standard protocol (contrast exposure risk, renal monitoring, low oral intake periods). Can revisit metformin reinstitution at discharge when renal function stable and patient reliably eating. HbA1c was 7.1% prior to transplant — reasonable control for a type 2 diabetic; anticipate glucose will be easier to manage once patient is ambulatory and eating full diet at home. Endocrine to be notified if persistent hyperglycemia.

9. PSYCHOSOCIAL SUPPORT
Patient is anxious but in good spirits overall, buoyed by the news of engraftment. Her husband is present and highly supportive. Social work has been involved throughout this hospitalization and will coordinate post-discharge support. Nurse navigator to discuss home care plan. Patient is a nurse herself and has been engaged in understanding her own care, which has been a strength throughout this illness.

10. DISCHARGE PLANNING
With neutrophil engraftment now confirmed and patient clinically stable, we are beginning active discharge planning. Target discharge within the next 2-3 days once platelet engraftment criterion is met (or near-met), patient is eating well, and no new complications arise. Patient and caregiver will receive education on:
- Daily temperature monitoring and when to call/present to clinic
- Central line care at home (Hickman)
- Tacrolimus administration, importance of consistent timing, and side effect recognition
- Medication adherence including antifungal, antiviral, and PCP prophylaxis
- Activity restrictions: avoid crowds and those with infections; food safety precautions (neutropenic diet can be liberalized gradually per institutional guidelines)
- Outpatient BMT clinic follow-up: within 2-3 days of discharge for CBC check, tacrolimus level, and wound check

OVERALL IMPRESSION: Day +18 post-MUD RIC-alloHSCT is proceeding very well. Neutrophil engraftment is confirmed as of day +16. No veno-occlusive disease, no acute GVHD, no CMV reactivation, no active infection. LFTs and renal function normal. Tacrolimus therapeutic. We are cautiously optimistic. Discharge anticipated within 2-3 days pending platelet engraftment and continued clinical stability.

Cancer present: NO. Disease status: MRD-negative CR1 maintained. Post-engraftment monitoring to continue per protocol.

---
Electronically signed: BMT Attending, Hematology/Oncology
Co-signed: BMT Fellow
